commit.message:
fix: encryption plugin not stripping type-specific schema keywords from encrypted fields (#8237)

The encryption plugin's schema transformation only removed `properties` from
encrypted field definitions but left other type-specific keywords like
`maxLength`, `required`, `items`, `enum`, etc. This caused validation errors
when using a validator storage (e.g. wrappedValidateAjvStorage) inside the
encryption storage wrapper.

Fix: replace the entire encrypted field schema with `{ type: 'string' }`
instead of only changing the type and deleting `properties`.

Added two test cases:
- Encrypted string field with maxLength (fails without fix due to ciphertext
  exceeding maxLength validation)
- Encrypted object field with required sub-properties (fails without fix due
  to AJV strict mode rejecting leftover `required` keyword on type `string`)

Fix index string generation for negative decimal numbers (#8231)

* fix: correct index string generation for negative decimal numbers

The getNumberIndexString function and its inlined variant in
getIndexableStringMonad used Math.abs(fieldValue) to extract the
decimal part of numbers. For negative numbers, this produced
reversed ordering within the same floor group. For example,
-1.9 would sort after -1.1 instead of before.

Fix: use (fieldValue - Math.floor(fieldValue)) to compute the
fractional part, which gives the correct offset from the floor
value regardless of sign.

Add test case to verify correct sort order for negative decimals.

commit.message:
fix: rateQueryPlan checked startKeys twice instead of endKeys (copy-paste bug) (#8230)

* fix: prevent TypeError crash in isFindOneByIdQuery when primary key selector value is null

When a query selector contained a null value for the primary key field
(e.g., {selector: {id: null}}), isFindOneByIdQuery() would crash with
a TypeError trying to access null.$eq. The fix adds a null/type guard
before accessing properties on the selector value, so non-object values
like null safely fall through to return false (standard query path).

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rateQueryPlan evaluated startKeys twice instead of endKeys (copy-paste bug)

rateQueryPlan() in query-planner.ts line 370 used queryPlan.startKeys when
computing nonMaxKeyCount, but should have used queryPlan.endKeys. This was
a copy-paste error from line 367 that made both checks identical, causing
the query planner to ignore endKey quality when rating index plans.

This caused the planner to fail to prefer indexes that covered $lt/$lte
selectors, since those operators only constrain endKeys (not startKeys).
For example, a query with {age: {$lt: 40}} would pick an index without
'age' instead of one with 'age', because the endKey constraint was never
evaluated.

Updated the rx-storage-query-correctness test expectation for the $lt
sort-by-non-selector test from selectorSatisfiedByIndex:false to true,
since the planner now correctly selects the index that covers the selector.

commit.message:
Fix docsDataMap mutation in event-reduce causing missing documents after insert-delete cycles (#8228)

* fix: prevent docsDataMap mutation in calculateNewResults and add tests

The event-reduce calculateNewResults function was passing the cached
docsDataMap by reference to runAction, which mutates the map in-place.
This corrupts the cached map when a later event triggers runFullQueryAgain
and the full re-exec returns unchanged results (keeping the old result).
On subsequent event-reduce calls, insertAtSortPosition would check
keyDocumentMap.has(docId) and incorrectly skip insertions for documents
that were in the corrupted map but not in the actual results.

Fix: copy the map with new Map() before passing to runAction.

Also adds comprehensive tests for reactive query observation edge cases.

commit.message:
fix(modify): deep-clone data before passing to modifier to prevent shared reference corruption (#8232)

* fix(modify): deep-clone data before passing to modifier to prevent shared reference corruption

The non-incremental modify() method was passing this._data directly to
modifierFromPublicToInternal without deep-cloning. stripMetaDataFromDocument
only creates a shallow copy, so nested objects were shared references with the
document's internal _data. In production mode (without dev-mode deep-freezing),
a modifier function could corrupt the document's internal state by mutating
nested objects through these shared references.

This is inconsistent with patch() which deep-clones, and incrementalModify()
which deep-clones via the write queue.

The fix adds clone(oldData) in modify() before passing to
modifierFromPublicToInternal, matching the behavior of the other methods.

commit.message:
Clone non-primitive default values in fillObjectWithDefaults to prevent shared references (#8229)

* fix: clone non-primitive default values in fillObjectWithDefaults to prevent shared references

Default values from the schema (arrays, objects) were assigned by reference
in fillObjectWithDefaults. This meant all documents using a default shared
the same mutable reference. If any code (e.g. a pre-insert hook) mutated
a default array/object, it would corrupt the schema's cached defaults,
causing subsequent inserts to receive incorrect default values.

Fix: shallow-clone arrays (via .slice()) and objects (via spread) when
assigning defaults so each document gets an independent copy.

runtime ARN (arn:...:runtime/xxx) but WebSocket presigned URLs evaluate
against the full endpoint ARN (arn:...:runtime/xxx/runtime-endpoint/DEFAULT).

but was not backported to the deploy script template.
